why: `described_class` adds obscurity because the reader cannot tell which class is under test without context; the actual class name makes it self-documenting.
"POST /orders" is a code token (HTTP verb + path) that names the mechanism, not the scenario's meaning; it doesn't say what the group of tests is about from the user's perspective.
"works" is maximally vague — it doesn't describe any scenario or expected outcome, so the test name gives no information about what is being specified.
suggested_fix: it "creates an order with the given SKU" do

why: `.last` depends on insertion order; if any other code path creates an order (a before-hook, a factory, another test leaking state) this silently retrieves the wrong record.
suggested_fix:
expect { post orders_path, params: { sku: "ABC-1", qty: 2 } }
  .to change { Order.where(sku: "ABC-1").count }.by(1)

offending_code:
gateway = instance_double(PaymentGateway, charge: true)
allow(PaymentGateway).to receive(:new).and_return(gateway)

post orders_path, params: { sku: "ABC-1", qty: 2 }

expect(gateway).to have_received(:charge).with(amount: 1998)
why: `have_received(:charge)` asserts that a specific method was invoked — it tests the means (the implementation) rather than the observable end (the customer was charged). If the controller is refactored to call a different method or delegate charging differently, this test breaks even if the customer is still charged correctly.
suggested_fix:
# If a Charge record is persisted:
expect { post orders_path, params: { sku: "ABC-1", qty: 2 } }
  .to change { Charge.where(amount_cents: 1998).count }.by(1)

# Or, if using a test-mode gateway that records calls:
expect(PaymentGateway.test_charges.last[:amount]).to eq(1998)

offending_code:
gateway = instance_double(PaymentGateway, charge: true)
allow(PaymentGateway).to receive(:new).and_return(gateway)
why: Stubbing `PaymentGateway.new` couples the test to the controller's internal instantiation mechanism: if the controller switches to `PaymentGateway.for_environment` or a dependency-injected collaborator, the test breaks without the behavior changing.
suggested_fix:
# Prefer a test-mode gateway configured at the class level rather than
# intercepting .new in the test:
PaymentGateway.test_mode!

post orders_path, params: { sku: "ABC-1", qty: 2 }

# Then assert on the observable result (see observable-not-method-calls)
